tool_args.label: License Agreement with Town of Oyster Bay for Hicksville DRI at Hicksville Station
tool_args.raw_text: License agreement with the Town of Oyster Bay for use of two parking lots in connection with the Hicksville Downtown Revitalization Initiative at Hicksville Station

payload.event_name: License Agreement with Town of Oyster Bay for Hicksville DRI
payload.description: License agreement with the Town of Oyster Bay for use of two parking lots (69,533 sq ft and 30,899 sq ft) at Hicksville Station for construction of a pedestrian passageway, public open space, and festival plaza in connection with the Hicksville Downtown Revitalization Initiative. Term: 1 year with automatic annual renewals. Compensation: None.

tool_args.label: Grant of Permanent Easement to Brookhaven Logistics Center for Sewer Connection in Yaphank, NY
tool_args.raw_text: Grant of permanent easement agreement to Brookhaven Logistics Center, LLC for a sewer connection in Yaphank, NY

payload.event_name: Grant of Permanent Easement to Brookhaven Logistics Center
payload.description: Grant of subsurface permanent easement of approximately 1,360 sq ft at 205 Sills Road, Yaphank, NY for installation of a sanitary sewer force main crossing LIRR property. Term: Perpetual. Compensation: $11,000 plus reimbursement for MTA's appraisal and legal fees.

tool_args.label: License of Land with Ellen Kirwin and Matt Kirwin at Former Shinnecock Hills Station, Southampton, NY
tool_args.raw_text: License of land with Ellen Kirwin and Matt Kirwin for a residential use in Southampton, NY

payload.event_name: License of Land with Ellen Kirwin and Matt Kirwin
payload.description: License of approximately 15,700 sq ft at former Shinnecock Hills Station, 100 Hills Station Rd, Southampton, NY for use of yard areas and land under structures (year-round primary residence). Term: 10 years. Compensation: $3,768 per annum ($314/month) with 3% annual increases ($0.24 per sq ft).

tool_args.label: License of Land with Hawkins Ave Development RHP2 LLC for Recreational Use in Ronkonkoma, NY
tool_args.raw_text: License of land with Hawkins Ave Development RHP2 LLC for a recreational use in Ronkonkoma, NY

payload.event_name: License of Land with Hawkins Ave Development RHP2 LLC
payload.description: License of approximately 4,300 sq ft strip of land between LIRR's Ronkonkoma Station garage and Ronkonkoma Hub Development Property for recreational activity (dog run). Term: 10 years.

tool_args.label: Hicksville Downtown Revitalization Initiative
tool_args.raw_text: the Town was awarded a grant for $8.7 million by the New York Department of State, under the NYS Downtown Revitalization Initiative to fund the design and construction of the Town's Hicksville Downtown Revitalization Initiative

payload.description: Town of Oyster Bay initiative to construct a new pedestrian passageway, public open space, and festival plaza connecting Hicksville Station to Broadway and the Hicksville business district, including trees, seating, and a temporary pavilion connecting to Kennedy Park.
payload.location: Hicksville Station, Town of Oyster Bay, Nassau County, New York

location_normalized.raw_text: Hicksville Station, Town of Oyster Bay, Nassau County, New York

tool_args.label: Hicksville license bypasses public offering per Circumstance 4
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Real Estate is authorized to forego a public offering and enter into an agreement directly with the Town, pursuant to the MTA Real Estate Department's Policy and Procedures for the Licensing of Real Property under Circumstance 4, which provides for entering into license agreements with municipalities for municipal purposes.

payload.claim_text: MTA Real Estate is authorized to forego a public offering and enter into an agreement directly with the Town, pursuant to the MTA Real Estate Department's Policy and Procedures for the Licensing of Real Property under Circumstance 4, which provides for entering into license agreements with municipalities for municipal purposes.

tool_args.label: Yaphank easement uses Public Authorities Law Section 2897(6)(c)
tool_args.raw_text: Public Authorities Law Section 2897(6)(c) allows public authorities to dispose of property by negotiation rather than by public bidding in certain circumstances, including where the value of the property to be disposed of does not exceed $15,000, which is the situation in this case.

payload.claim_text: Public Authorities Law Section 2897(6)(c) allows public authorities to dispose of property by negotiation rather than by public bidding in certain circumstances, including where the value of the property to be disposed of does not exceed $15,000, which is the situation in this case.

tool_args.label: Kirwin license bypasses public offering per Circumstance 2
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Real Estate is authorized to forego a public offering and enter into an agreement directly with the Licensee pursuant to the MTA Real Estate Department's Policy and Procedures for the Licensing of Real Property under Circumstance 2, where the market rental value of the space, determined using standard practices, is less than $5,000 per year or $10.00 per square foot, whichever is greater. For this transaction the value per square foot is .24¢.

payload.claim_text: MTA Real Estate is authorized to forego a public offering and enter into an agreement directly with the Licensee pursuant to the MTA Real Estate Department's Policy and Procedures for the Licensing of Real Property under Circumstance 2, where the market rental value of the space, determined using standard practices, is less than $5,000 per year or $10.00 per square foot, whichever is greater. For this transaction the value per square foot is .24¢.

For this transaction the value per square foot is $2.25.

payload.claim_text: MTA Real Estate is authorized to forego a public offering and enter into an agreement directly with Licensee, pursuant to the MTA Real Estate Department's Policy and Procedures for the Licensing of Real Property under Circumstance 2, where the market rental value of the space, determined using standard practices, is less than $5,000 per year or $10.00 per square foot, whichever is greater. For this transaction the value per square foot is $2.25.
